content: sd plan submit takes 0-based step indices in the 'blocks' arrays (e.g. step 0 with blocks:[1] means step 0 blocks step 1); 'sd plan show' renders them 1-based for humans. Validation error 'step index N is out of range (have N steps)' means the index equals or exceeds the step count — convert 1-based draft to 0-based before submit. Verified against pl-2f15 (warren R-06 cron scheduler plan, 8 steps).

title: pi as third built-in runtime: parity-first phased plan (pl-4374)
rationale: Picked phased landing (parity → customization → multi-provider → cost → event-model → docs) over a single mega-PR. Cross-repo burrow piRuntime is step 0 blocker for all warren-side work. Extensions deferred until canopy ships an artifact type (TS source in canopy sections is awkward). RPC mode chosen over JSON mode for bidirectional steer/abort/get_session_stats surface. Cost columns (cost_usd + tokens_*) trust pi's get_session_stats verbatim; warren-side price tables rejected. supportsResume=true (pi has --continue/--session); not codex-shaped.

name: pi runtime external research (pi-mono / @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent)
description: Pi is a TS coding-agent toolkit (badlogic/pi-mono, earendil-works/pi). CLI: pi (-p print, --mode json|rpc, --provider, --model, --thinking, -c/--continue, --session <path|id>, --skill, --prompt-template, -e/--extension). Multi-provider: Anthropic/OpenAI/Google/Bedrock/Vertex/DeepSeek/Groq/Mistral/Azure + custom via ~/.pi/agent/models.json. RPC commands include prompt/steer/abort/get_state/get_session_stats/get_messages/set_model/cycle_model/new_session/switch_session/fork/clone/bash/compact. Event kinds: agent_start/end, turn_start/end, message_start/update/end, tool_execution_start/update/end, queue_update, compaction_start/end, auto_retry_start/end, extension_error. get_session_stats returns {tokens:{input,output,cacheRead,cacheWrite,total}, cost (USD), contextUsage:{tokens,contextWindow,percent}}. Customization: .pi/skills/<name>/SKILL.md (agentskills.io standard), .pi/prompts/<name>.md (template vars {{var}}), .pi/extensions/*.ts (registerTool, registerCommand via ExtensionAPI). Sessions persist at ~/.pi/agent/sessions/. Docs: pi.dev/docs/latest/usage and github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/blob/main/packages/coding-agent/docs/rpc.md.

title: R-19 preview plan starts with SPEC §11.L design lock + burrow inbound-networking coord seed
rationale: R-19 is filed [proposed] / needs-sketching in ROADMAP: the sketch touches burrow networking policy, warren proxy + routing, TLS / wildcard certs, signed-cookie auth, and TTL+LRU lifecycle. pl-2c59 makes step 1 (SPEC §11.L + a burrow-side seed for inbound networking) a gate on every other step so the design lands before code, mirroring how pl-5d74 (R-02) and pl-49f3 (V1 phased plan) deferred consumption to follow-up seeds when the schema seam wasn't yet stable. Same-sandbox default + signed-cookie auth + port-allocator-in-runs-table are picked here per ROADMAP open-question prose; R-12 cross-host routing is explicitly deferred. Reap-time launch is the 5th best-effort sub-step, reusing the pr_open pattern (mx-05abb2) so a preview failure never fails the run.

title: Frame R-13 as async-repo refactor first, then add Postgres backend
rationale: bun-sqlite (sync) → node-postgres (async) is the bigger blast radius (~51 sync call sites across 7 repos + ~30 test files), not the dialect mapping. Plan pl-f17e lands step 1 as a pure-refactor PR with SQLite still as the only backend so any regression is bisectable against mechanical changes. ROADMAP R-13's 'Drizzle Kit emit both dialects from one schema file' sketch is approximate — drizzle-orm has no shared sqlite/pg table builder, so the plan calls for parallel src/db/schema/{sqlite,postgres}.ts files backed by a shared src/db/schema/columns.ts constants module + a CI lint that diffs the two shapes. WARREN_DB_PATH stays as a back-compat alias that synthesizes a sqlite:// URL; WARREN_DB_URL is the new primary contract.

name: cross-repo-design-lock-seed
description: Phase 1 of any cross-repo plan freezes the design in SPEC.md §11.* AND files the dependency seed in the consumer repo's .seeds/ before any code lands. The warren-side SPEC names the upstream seed id (e.g. burrow-8647) inline so future readers navigate the seam without grepping; ROADMAP entry repeats the id at the bullet that depends on it. Used by warren-94d8 (R-19, plan pl-2c59): SPEC §11.L design lock + burrow-8647 filed before warren steps 3-6 could move.

title: V2 preview teardown reuses teardownPreview, does not extend EvictionReason
rationale: warren-38f7's seed framing was internally contradictory: it called for 'reuse the manual-teardown route' AND 'emit preview_evicted with reason=pr_closed'. The lock resolves toward teardownPreview because (a) the route's CAS already serializes against the eviction worker and manual route, so a webhook racing an idle-TTL sweep deterministically lands in exactly one; (b) preview_torn_down already carries a free-form actor field for attribution, so a structured tag ('github-webhook:pr_merged' / 'github-webhook:pr_closed_unmerged') distinguishes trigger source without growing the enum or adding a new event kind; (c) PR-close is an external trigger, not a periodic sweep, so it's semantically closer to manual teardown than to worker eviction. The seed framing predated the warren-d725 split between preview_torn_down (operator/external, carries actor) and preview_evicted (worker sweep, carries EvictionReason). Locking the design now (rather than waiting for webhook infra) keeps the eviction story in one place and prevents re-derivation when the receiver lands. SPEC §11.N is the canonical home; implementation seed warren-38f7 stays open as the tracker.

content: When a plan step's title or description includes a '(warren-XXXX fold-in)' marker (e.g. pl-f1be step warren-670f folding in warren-5549), close the folded-in seed in the same commit that closes the step, not just the step itself. Otherwise the folded seed lingers open until someone re-discovers it via 'sd ready'. The marker means 'this step subsumes the work of seed XXXX' — closing only the step leaves the parent seed dangling and forces a future agent to verify the work was actually done before closing.

When a ROADMAP R-XX ships, update its status line in-place with 'Status: [shipped] — landed via plan pl-XXXX (parent seed warren-XXXX), YYYY-MM-DD.' then a concrete summary of what shipped (migration filenames, new routes, schema PK changes, frontmatter labels, UI surfaces, acceptance scenario number). Convert the 'Open questions.' subsection to 'Follow-ups (filed as seeds; not in V1).' once those questions are filed as seeds in the plan's docs step. Examples: R-02 (pl-5d74), R-06 (pl-2f15), R-03 (pl-fef5).
